step.title: Step 67
step.actionDisplay: LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT -> LEFT

sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 20 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions (first 10): LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 159, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Green 2 at (57-62, 30-32) can move LEFT when below the purple horizontal strip. The green has crossed the purple vertical strip and is now in the bottom-left quadrant. Can now navigate to Dark outline 1.
- Plan: Move LEFT to reach col 9, then UP to reach row 9. Try smaller batches to avoid interruptions.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 75 of about 109 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Hmm, the green is still at (57-62, 30-32) after 20 LEFT actions. It didn't move! This is strange. Earlier, 1 LEFT action moved the green from (57-62, 33-35) to (57-62, 30-32). Now, 20 LEFT actions didn't move the green.
Wait, looking at the output, only 10 LEFT actions were executed (the output says "Executed actions (first 10): LEFT, LEFT, ..."). But the green is still at (57-62, 30-32). So the 10 LEFT actions didn't move the green.
This means the green is blocked from moving LEFT. But earlier, 1 LEFT action moved the green from (57-62, 33-35) to (57-62, 30-32). So the green can move LEFT once, but then it's blocked.
Looking at the image, the green is at the left edge of the bottom-left quadrant. The left edge of the board is col 0, and the green is at cols 30-32. So there's plenty of space to the left.
Wait, but the green didn't move. Maybe the green hit a wall or obstacle at col 30.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, the green is at the bottom of the board, near the HUD bar. The green is at rows 57-62, cols 30-32. The left edge of the gray region in the bottom-left quadrant is col 0, and the right edge is col 32 (the purple vertical strip).
So the green is at cols 30-32, which is near the right edge of the bottom-left quadrant. Moving LEFT should move it to cols 29-31, 28-30, etc.
But the green didn't move. Maybe the green is blocked by something.
Wait, let me re-examine. The green was at (57-62, 33-35) before the LEFT action. After 1 LEFT action, it moved to (57-62, 30-32). That's a shift of 3 columns (33-30=3, 35-32=3). But 1 LEFT action should only move it by 1 column.
This is the same issue I noticed earlier. The green seems to move by 3 columns per LEFT action, not 1 column.
So after 1 LEFT action, the green moved from cols 33-35 to cols 30-32. Now, if I try to move LEFT again, the green would move from cols 30-32 to cols 27-29. But the green didn't move.
Maybe the green is blocked at cols 30-32. Or maybe the green can only move LEFT when it's at cols 33-35 (in the bottom-right quadrant), but not when it's at cols 30-32 (in the bottom-left quadrant).
Actually, wait. The purple vertical strip is at cols 32-34. The green was at cols 33-35, which overlaps the purple strip. After 1 LEFT action, the green moved to cols 30-32, which is to the left of the purple strip.
Maybe the green can only move LEFT when it's overlapping the purple strip, but not when it's entirely in the gray region.
Or maybe the green hit the left edge of the purple vertical strip and can't move further LEFT.
Let me try moving UP from the current position. The green is at (57-62, 30-32), which is in the bottom-left quadrant. Moving UP should move it towards the top-left quadrant.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Green 2 at (57-62, 30-32) is blocked from moving LEFT. The green moved from (57-62, 33-35) to (57-62, 30-32) with 1 LEFT action (3 columns shift), but now can't move LEFT further. The green is in the bottom-left quadrant.
Plan: Try moving UP from the current position to navigate towards Dark outline 1.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try UP from current position
result = action(['UP'] * 10)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'W') and n['pixels'] <= 32:
        print(f"  color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} bound={n['boundary']}")
print("  board_changed:", result.get('board_changed'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
